jokes.text: Doctor is a prestigious occupation...but it's one of the only jobs...where you have to have your diploma right up there on the wall. It makes them seem so insecure, doesn't it? 'I really am a doctor, you know. If you think I'm not, just check it out.'
jokes.setup: Observing that doctors display diplomas prominently on walls
jokes.punchline: Reframing this as insecurity and desperation for validation
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with sharp insight about professional insecurity

jokes.text: 'Go in that room, take your pants off, wait 15 minutes...then I'll give you my opinion.' After that, anybody with pants on...seems like they know what they're talking about. In any difference of opinion, pants always beats no pants.
jokes.setup: Doctor's psychological power tactics with patients
jokes.punchline: Reducing all authority to the simple binary of pants vs. no pants
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from specific observation to universal absurd principle. 'Pants always beats no pants' is perfectly quotable

jokes.text: Can I say one word to you? Lobster.
jokes.setup: George's dramatic buildup to 'one word'
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic word is just 'Lobster'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's food obsession, but fairly simple structure

jokes.text: You know, you're the only woman I've never thought about the price. Get the lobster. I beg you to get it.
jokes.setup: George's romantic declaration about not thinking about price
jokes.punchline: Immediately begging her to order expensive lobster
jokes.explanation: Perfect George character moment - romantic gesture immediately undermined by cheapness

jokes.text: George...can you ever forgive me? Have you decided yet? Yes. I'll have the lobster.
jokes.setup: Woman asks emotional question about forgiveness during breakup
jokes.punchline: George thinks she's asking about the lobster order
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect timing. George's obliviousness to emotional moment is peak character comedy

jokes.text: You know, I'm starting to think that maybe lobster isn't the way to go.
jokes.setup: George just got dumped after obsessing over lobster
jokes.punchline: Now questions the lobster choice rather than the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect George logic - blaming the food choice rather than acknowledging relationship issues

jokes.text: Anyone can get into podiatry school. George got into podiatry school.
jokes.setup: Jerry dismissing podiatry as less prestigious than medicine
jokes.punchline: Using George as the ultimate example of low standards
jokes.explanation: Brutal but efficient insult that uses George as the measuring stick for incompetence

jokes.text: Well, we can't all be dating podiatrists.
jokes.setup: Elaine bragging about dating a doctor
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry dismissal of podiatrist status
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan delivery but relatively simple structure

jokes.text: Looks like a swan.
jokes.setup: Jerry examining George's lobster
jokes.punchline: Comparing elegant swan to the defeated lobster George ordered
jokes.explanation: Simple visual observation without much comedic depth

jokes.text: Her parents won't let her date anyone who isn't Latvian Orthodox! Latvian Orthodox?
jokes.setup: Religious restriction on dating
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and obscure denomination
jokes.explanation: Excellent choice of absurd denomination - specific enough to be funny, obscure enough to be ridiculous

jokes.text: It is lobster. She's limiting herself to Latvian Orthodox? This was the only woman I never lied to.
jokes.setup: George lamenting the religious restriction
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lies to every other woman
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing George's casual dishonesty in relationships

jokes.text: To Latvian Orthodox? Why not? What do I care? You know, it's not like changing toothpaste.
jokes.setup: George considering religious conversion for a woman
jokes.punchline: Jerry's comparison to changing toothpaste brands
jokes.explanation: Perfect analogy highlighting the absurdity - religion reduced to consumer choice level

jokes.text: It's like Edward VIII...abdicating the throne to marry Mrs. Simpson. Like King Edward, Jerry. Yeah, well...King Edward didn't live in Queens with Frank and Estelle Costanza.
jokes.setup: George comparing himself to romantic King Edward VIII
jokes.punchline: Jerry's brutal reality check about living with his parents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of George's grandiose comparison. The specific mention of Frank and Estelle makes it devastatingly funny

jokes.text: You make a little contribution, you have a ceremony. I am gonna think about this. I am really gonna think about this.
jokes.setup: George oversimplifying religious conversion
jokes.punchline: His determined repetition like it's a business decision
jokes.explanation: Good George character moment showing his impulsive decision-making process

jokes.text: As a kid, while kids were out playing, I'd be in my room practicing kissing.
jokes.setup: Elaine being complimented on kissing
jokes.punchline: Revealing obsessive childhood practice routine
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment revealing Elaine's competitive approach to romance

jokes.text: Fungicide? Fungus?
jokes.setup: Jerry discovering mysterious medicine in cabinet
jokes.punchline: His horrified realization about what it treats
jokes.explanation: Sets up the fungus mystery but relatively simple reaction

jokes.text: I was not snooping. I did not break the seal. There was no breaking and entering. I wouldn't do that. I always open medicine cabinets.
jokes.setup: Jerry defending himself against snooping accusations
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting he always opens medicine cabinets
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction structure showing Jerry's weak moral boundaries

jokes.text: But that's what podiatrists do. They deal in fungus. They're knee-deep in fungus. This guy knows fungus.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Jerry ask the podiatrist about fungus
jokes.punchline: Escalating to 'knee-deep in fungus' imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalating imagery with 'knee-deep in fungus' being memorably gross

jokes.text: Fungi. What? Fungi.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying 'funguses'
jokes.punchline: Kramer's pedantic correction to proper plural
jokes.explanation: Simple grammar correction joke elevated slightly by Kramer's character

jokes.text: I think the hats.
jokes.setup: Father asking what aspect of faith attracts George
jokes.punchline: George's shallow focus on religious headwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - reducing profound spiritual question to fashion preference

jokes.text: The hat conveys that solemn religious look you want in a faith. Very pious.
jokes.setup: George explaining his hat attraction
jokes.punchline: Treating religious devotion like a fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Continues the shallow religious reasoning with consumer-like evaluation

jokes.text: But I know the basic plot, yeah.
jokes.setup: Asked about Orthodox theology knowledge
jokes.punchline: Reducing religion to a 'plot' like a movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reduction of theology to entertainment - perfectly captures George's shallow approach

jokes.text: Plot? Yes, you know, the flood...and the lepers and the commandments and all that.
jokes.setup: Father's confusion about 'plot' description
jokes.punchline: George doubling down with 'and all that' dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing George's casual dismissal of sacred stories

jokes.text: So...am I in?
jokes.setup: Long theological discussion about conversion process
jokes.punchline: George treating it like club membership
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing George's impatience with spiritual process

jokes.text: Don't you offer any kind of an express conversion? A quick change?
jokes.setup: George told he needs to study religious texts
jokes.punchline: Asking for express religious conversion like fast food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern consumer mentality applied to ancient spiritual practice

jokes.text: Mine.
jokes.setup: Kramer saying meeting her was 'a pleasure'
jokes.punchline: Sister's suggestive single-word response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for the nun attraction subplot

jokes.text: And what is Brother Costanza planning on telling Mother Costanza?
jokes.setup: George announcing he'll be Brother Costanza
jokes.punchline: Kramer's alliterative question about his mother
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay highlighting the absurdity of George becoming religious

jokes.text: Brother Costanza will be taking the vow of silence.
jokes.setup: Question about telling his mother
jokes.punchline: George's plan to avoid the conversation entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect George solution - using religion to avoid difficult family conversation

jokes.text: I think she liked me. What do you mean, she liked you? Liked me. Kramer, they like everybody. They're friendly people. I think I picked up on a vibe.
jokes.setup: Kramer thinks nun likes him romantically
jokes.punchline: Jerry's reality check about nuns being generally friendly
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Kramer's delusional romantic subplot

jokes.text: You picked up on a vibe from a nun. Yeah. Jerry, I'm telling you, I have this power...and I have no control over it.
jokes.setup: Jerry's incredulous response about nun vibe
jokes.punchline: Kramer's grandiose claim about uncontrollable power over women
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer delusion moment setting up the kavorca storyline

jokes.text: How do you even know it's hers? Maybe it belonged to Carol. Did you see a name on the tube? I didn't look. Well, take a look. It might not even belong to her.
jokes.setup: Jerry worried about girlfriend's fungus medicine
jokes.punchline: Kramer's logical suggestion to check the name
jokes.explanation: Functional plot advancement but not particularly funny

jokes.text: No, no. I'm just saying, you didn't really go to medical school. You went to podiatry school...which I'm sure is very grueling in its own way.
jokes.setup: Podiatrist correcting Elaine about being a doctor
jokes.punchline: Elaine's condescending 'grueling in its own way' dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with backhanded compliment structure

jokes.text: I went to podiatry school because I like feet. I chose to work with feet.
jokes.setup: Defending his profession choice
jokes.punchline: Overly passionate declaration of foot love
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment but relatively predictable foot fetish implication

jokes.text: George? What are you doing in there? What? Nothing. You've been in there an hour. Nothing. George, open the door. No. Georgie. No!
jokes.setup: George mysteriously locked in bathroom for an hour
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate refusal to come out
jokes.explanation: Good escalating tension but setup for later reveal rather than standalone joke

jokes.text: And I'll need that in the form of a question.
jokes.setup: George studying for conversion test
jokes.punchline: Jerry treating it like Jeopardy game show
jokes.explanation: Solid game show reference applied to religious education

jokes.text: Well, not on purpose. I was...I was hoping there'd be a name on the tube.
jokes.setup: Accused of stealing medicine
jokes.punchline: Jerry's weak justification for theft
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Jerry's moral flexibility

jokes.text: Yes. He's a doctor.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if he should apologize to podiatrist
jokes.punchline: Kramer now calling him a doctor after earlier dismissals
jokes.explanation: Simple callback but lacks the punch of original joke

jokes.text: What, you're cheating on your conversion test?
jokes.setup: Finding George with cheat sheet for religious test
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of cheating on spiritual examination
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist concept - treating spiritual conversion like academic cheating

jokes.text: All this power! Look what I'm doing. I'm dangerous, Jerry. I'm very, very dangerous!
jokes.setup: Kramer claiming Sister wants to leave Church for him
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-assessment as dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer delusion moment with dramatic escalation

jokes.text: Oh, I'm full of it, Father.
jokes.setup: Father saying George is filled with spirit of the Lord
jokes.punchline: George's double entendre response
jokes.explanation: Excellent double entendre with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: It is a Latvian word which means 'the lure of the animal.'
jokes.setup: Father explaining Kramer's mysterious power
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific mystical concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant invented mystical concept that becomes series mythology

jokes.text: Women are drawn to you. They would give anything to be possessed by you. Help me, Father. Help me!
jokes.setup: Father explaining kavorca power
jokes.punchline: Kramer's dramatic plea for help with his 'curse'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Kramer's delusion with religious validation

jokes.text: Hey, what the hell is going on? It stinks. What are you doing? I've got the kavorca. The kavorca? What is that? The lure of the animal. I'm dangerous.
jokes.setup: Jerry encountering smelly Kramer
jokes.punchline: Kramer's matter-of-fact explanation of his condition
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Kramer treating mystical curse as medical condition

jokes.text: You know, you're funkifying the whole building. Keep away, Jerry. Just keep away.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about Kramer's smell
jokes.punchline: Jerry's invented word 'funkifying' and Kramer's dramatic warning
jokes.explanation: Good neologism with 'funkifying' and Kramer's theatrical response

jokes.text: Wait. Is this the group that goes around mutilating squirrels?
jokes.setup: Frank learning about George's conversion
jokes.punchline: His bizarre specific concern about squirrel mutilation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frank Costanza moment - completely unexpected and specific absurd concern

jokes.text: I hardly slept last night with all the scratching. Bonkers was going crazy. Bonkers? My cat. He's got this weird sort of skin condition. It's some type of fungus.
jokes.setup: Suggesting someone was scratching all night
jokes.punchline: Reveal that it's her cat with fungus, not her
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff resolving the fungus mystery with relief

jokes.text: I said 'faddah.' I...I meant 'Father.' Just a little bit nervous.
jokes.setup: George mispronouncing 'Father' during ceremony
jokes.punchline: His nervous over-explanation of the mistake
jokes.explanation: Good nervous George moment showing his anxiety about the conversion

jokes.text: Get away from me, you creep. Yes. Yes, it worked! Sister Roberta. I still got time to catch her.
jokes.setup: Kramer approaching woman on street
jokes.punchline: His vindication that the anti-kavorca treatment worked
jokes.explanation: Good resolution showing Kramer's twisted logic about romantic rejection

jokes.text: Him.
jokes.setup: Sister explaining why she's leaving the Church
jokes.punchline: Simple pointing gesture identifying Kramer
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing for major plot convergence moment

jokes.text: You know, because I love the foot. I'm a big fan of the foot. And that pinkie toe. Come on! How adorable is the pinkie toe? It's my favorite toe.
jokes.setup: Podiatrist defending his profession
jokes.punchline: Increasingly passionate declarations about feet and toes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of character's foot obsession but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Elaine, it's her cat. Her cat has the fungus. So I need the tube back.
jokes.setup: Jerry's relief about fungus source
jokes.punchline: Immediate need to return stolen medicine
jokes.explanation: Plot resolution moment but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Something's wrong. I don't feel the same lure. You don't? What have I..? I must return to the Church.
jokes.setup: Kramer realizing his kavorca power is gone
jokes.punchline: His dramatic decision to return to Church
jokes.explanation: Good character arc completion but not exceptionally funny

jokes.text: It's like, 'Here's a salve from 1983...some cream from the '70s.' But you want to keep it private. A medicine cabinet is a place that reveals our weaknesses...and can throw off the balance between people that are going out.
jokes.setup: Jerry's closing monologue about medicine cabinets
jokes.punchline: Deep analysis of bathroom psychology and dating power dynamics
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor tying episode themes together

jokes.text: Somebody peeks in there, 'Oh, I see Mr. Perfect needs...tough actin' Tinactin. Well, I guess I'll be calling the shots in this relationship from now on.'
jokes.setup: Medicine cabinet revealing weaknesses
jokes.punchline: Specific product name and power shift in relationship
jokes.explanation: Great specific product reference and relationship psychology insight

jokes.text: But I just don't feel ready to make a commitment yet. Maybe when I get back from Latvia. Latvia?
jokes.setup: George's conversion effort to win her back
jokes.punchline: She's moving to the country of the religion he converted to
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic twist ending - George's conversion becomes completely pointless in the most absurd way possible

jokes.text: I'd like a doggy bag for this, please.
jokes.setup: George's romantic evening completely ruined
jokes.punchline: Practical request to save the expensive lobster
jokes.explanation: Perfect George ending - even in romantic devastation, he's practical about the expensive food
